skills: client advisory in small financial firm environments
domain_experience: former compliance consultant for small financial firms
understands recurring compliance operations and exam-readiness workflows
assets: practical knowledge of compliance evidence trails
ability to define safe service boundaries
AI can accelerate summarization, extraction, and checklist generation
network_access: a few warm contacts in small financial firms
constraints: almost no desire to become a content creator
credibility_signals: former consultant in a regulated niche
domain fluency in small-firm compliance realities
unfair_advantages: knows where small firms have recurring documentation and evidence gaps
can distinguish operational support from legal advice
can sell through trust rather than audience-building

opportunities.title: Exam-readiness and evidence-pack sprint
opportunities.thesis: Sell a fixed-scope engagement to organize compliance evidence, issue logs, attestations, and remediation tracking before an audit, annual review, or regulator exam.
opportunities.why_user_can_win: The user already understands the real document trail and operational scramble in small financial firms, which reduces discovery, improves trust, and makes AI outputs more useful.
validation_plan: Pitch a fixed-fee sprint to warm contacts
Test whether evidence organization is urgent enough to buy now
Deliver first engagement manually with AI-assisted summaries
kill_criteria: buyers see it as too one-off to justify payment
delivery cannot fit weekly time limits

opportunities.title: Annual compliance calendar and remediation tracker setup
opportunities.thesis: Offer a productized setup service that creates a recurring compliance calendar, owner map, evidence checklist, and remediation tracker in the client's existing tools.
opportunities.why_user_can_win: The user knows the recurring obligations and handoff failures common in small firms, making the setup higher quality than a generic operations consultant could provide.
validation_plan: Show a sample tracker to warm contacts
Ask whether they would pay for implementation versus building internally
Test one setup project end to end
kill_criteria: buyers prefer to keep setup entirely internal
engagements are too small to justify effort

opportunities.title: Compliance ops template library for small financial firms
opportunities.thesis: Build a paid library of checklists, trackers, evidence-request lists, and annual review workflows tailored to a narrow firm segment.
opportunities.why_user_can_win: The templates would reflect actual consulting experience and be more practical than generic compliance downloads.
validation_plan: Test demand by offering templates as part of a paid service
See which artifacts clients reuse or ask for repeatedly
Only package after repeated paid usage
kill_criteria: clients do not value templates separate from implementation
distribution remains too weak without content

opportunities.title: Compliance evidence concierge to AI-assisted workflow tool
opportunities.thesis: Start with a recurring service that helps firms collect, normalize, summarize, and track compliance evidence and follow-ups, then productize the repeated workflow into a lightweight tool.
opportunities.why_user_can_win: The user's edge is knowing the repeated operational pain behind proving compliance, plus having enough niche trust to land early pilots without building an audience.
validation_plan: Write a tightly scoped offer focused on operations support, not legal advice
Interview 5 warm contacts and test willingness to pay for a pilot
Run one manual pilot using AI for summarization and evidence-gap extraction
tool_specs: intake form for firm profile and obligations
AI-assisted extraction of owners, dates, obligations, and evidence gaps
master compliance task and remediation tracker
human review layer before client delivery
kill_criteria: buyers expect outsourced compliance accountability or legal interpretation
workflow cannot be standardized across similar firms

opportunities.title: Regulatory change triage briefing service to subscription dashboard
opportunities.thesis: Provide small firms with practical change summaries and implementation checklists for selected regulatory updates, then convert those briefs into a subscription product.
opportunities.why_user_can_win: The user can filter important changes for small firms better than a generic AI assistant, but the offer risks drifting into legal interpretation.
validation_plan: Test whether buyers want summaries or only actionable operational support
Use narrow topic pilots with strong disclaimers
Watch closely for pressure to provide legal conclusions
kill_criteria: buyers treat the product as legal advice
legal guardrails make the offer too narrow to pay for

next_actions: Define one-page offer with explicit scope and exclusions
Target one narrow firm segment first, such as small RIAs
Create sample evidence checklist, remediation tracker, and monthly compliance calendar
Contact 5 warm contacts for feedback calls
Ask one qualified contact to pilot a fixed-fee setup plus optional retainer
